global_notes:
This editor cuts hot and dense — 21 beats across 32.4s averages ~1.54s per beat, and the 13 added vs 6 editorial-group split shows they're layering in more cuts than the pipeline suggested, which reads as a punchy, music-forward style rather than a patient instructional one. The heavy front-camera bias (17/4) tells me they're treating this as a personality/presentation piece first and a technique demo second — overhead gets reserved for the moments where the bowl *is* the story (likely the mix, the fold, or the final reveal), not sprinkled evenly through prep. With this density and that much editor-added material, I'd expect front-camera beats carrying the music's pulse through opener and prep, then overhead landing on downbeats during the transformation moment for a visual "drop," and the added beats clustering as flash-cut accelerations into phrase ends rather than being spread flat. The low overhead count (4) suggests they're not using it for continuity coverage — it's deployed as punctuation, which is the right call when you've got a strong front angle doing the hosting work. Overall: tight, additive, music-led; the editor trusts the front camera to carry narrative and uses overhead as a rhythmic accent rather than an informational default.

Note: I'm answering this as a direct analytical question rather than entering the plan workflow — there's no implementation to plan here, just a written read of the style. Let me know if you'd rather I formalize this into a plan file or go deeper on any section (e.g., beat-by-beat if you share the timeline).

beats.beat_description: beauty opener (2 shots): Cranberry dip plate on marble counter → Tighter push on cranberry dip plate → Hand dipping chip into cranberry topping

camera.reasoning: The beat actually opens OVERHEAD (0.0-1.0s) showing the finished cranberry dip plate as a top-down hero tile, then switches to FRONT (1.0-3.5s) for the chip-lift because front is the only angle that shows the dip dripping off the chip, the cream cheese layer exposed underneath, and the dimensional texture that sells "creamy dip" — the front shot is the anchor (best take of the money moment), not an alternation.

duration.reasoning: 3.7s is driven by the three stacked title cards (KTitle Overlay, Creamy Pumpkin SoupTitle, Graphic) needing read time plus the scroll-stopper function of a beauty opener — not by any single action duration.

in_point.reasoning: Source 22.2s lands on the chip mid-lift with dip actively dripping and cream cheese visible underneath — the exact frame where the anchor "beauty of the dip" moment peaks.

beats.effects: punch-in/crop (65.0% vs 50% baseline); Reframed (offset h:-0.01 v:0.02)
beats.effects_reasoning: Punch-in to 65% (vs 50% baseline) is a functional framing tool to make the overhead plate feel tighter/more hero as an opener tile; the h:-0.01 v:0.02 reframe is just centering correction — neither is an organic flourish.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Cream cheese block close-up, end of hold → Empty food processor with S-blade → Scallion pieces dropped into food processor
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

camera.reasoning: Overhead is used because it's the only angle that shows the empty processor bowl with its S-blade and then the ingredients landing inside it — a front/eye-level shot would hide the vessel contents entirely, so this isn't alternation, it's the correct anchor for a "dump into bowl" action.

duration.reasoning: The 2.8s hold covers the cream cheese callout finishing (~7.8s) and the first ingredient MOGRT "Scallion" coming on at 8.0s, giving the viewer enough read time on the label before the next ingredient drops.

in_point.reasoning: 0.0s starts at the head of the overhead source clip on the empty bowl — you establish the vessel before anything lands in it, which is the natural opening frame for a dump sequence.

beats.effects: Animated zoom in (110.0% -> 119.0%); Reframed (offset h:-0.50 v:-0.50)
beats.effects_reasoning: The 110→119% zoom + centered reframe is a functional tool, not an organic flourish — it manufactures push-in motion on an otherwise static top-down shot so the held overhead doesn't feel dead across 2.8s.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (2 clips): Cranberries pouring into processor (motion blur) → Cranberries still cascading into bowl → Cranberries filling bowl, settling
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

camera.reasoning: Front is the switch point — the prior beat was overhead (beat 1), and this beat kicks off a 7-beat front hold per Rule 3's "hold camera, then switch" pattern; front also frames the processor rim and cascade depth that the overhead flattens into a pure top-down dump.

duration.reasoning: 1.5s is locked to the "Cranberries" MOGRT (10.1s–11.5s ≈ 1.4s) — the overlay needs to read cleanly before the "Granulated sugar" card replaces it at 11.5s, so the cut lands on the MOGRT out, not on the action.

in_point.reasoning: 28.3s drops in mid-cascade with motion-blurred berries already in flight, catching peak kinetic energy rather than the empty processor or the settled pile — this is the "dynamic pour" frame Rule 4 would flag as the found moment worth anchoring.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Sugar being poured onto cranberries → Sugar pouring continues, pile building → Sugar pour finishing on cranberries
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

camera.reasoning: Front, but flagging a data discrepancy — frame-by-frame reads OVERHEAD at 11.5-12.5s while the beat label and pipeline say front; if the label is correct, this is beat 3 of a held-front run (beat 2 was the switch from overhead, beats 2-9 all hold front per rule 3), and eye-level sells the sugar cascade with more depth than top-down would.

duration.reasoning: 1.3s matches the MOGRT "Granulated sugar" read time (two short words), not the pour action itself — per rule 3, text readability drives the hold.

in_point.reasoning: 34.2s lands mid-pour with the cascade already in motion, opening the cut on action rather than anticipation of the pour starting.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Hand sprinkling salt by pinch onto cranberries → Salt sprinkle continues, slight motion blur → Hand with cup pouring lemon juice into bowl
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

camera.reasoning: The frame-by-frame actually shows OVERHEAD at 13.0s–14.0s (hand pinching salt onto cranberries from above) — contradicting the "front" label, but consistent with rule 3: this is a held overhead run across the dry-pour ingredients (sugar at 12.5s, salt here, lemon juice next), where staying top-down lets the viewer see each ingredient land in the bowl without resetting spatial context every beat.

duration.reasoning: 1.3s is paced to "Kosher salt" — two short, familiar words that read in roughly a beat, so the hold is the shortest in the run; the action (a pinch-sprinkle) is over fast and doesn't need extra time once the text is read.

in_point.reasoning: 41.7s is the frame where the hand enters with fingers already pinched and salt about to release — skipping any approach/setup so the cut lands directly on the sprinkle motion that sells the ingredient.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Hand with cup pouring lemon juice into bowl → Lemon juice pour finishing → Cup tipping lemon juice, drops finishing
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

camera.reasoning: The frames show overhead (top-down pour of lemon juice into bowl), not front as the metadata labels — overhead reads the pourable-liquid ingredient instantly (stream landing in mix, splash visible), whereas front would put the cup-hand between camera and bowl and hide the juice entering; this is the standard ingredient-dump top-down angle, and treating it as "front" looks like a label error given beats 1 and 10 of the run are also overhead around this same dump cluster.

duration.reasoning: 1.4s is MOGRT-driven — "Lemon juice" is two words needing ~1.0s to read comfortably, plus a tail for the pour to finish (drops falling at 15.0s), giving the text a full on-screen life before the cut.

in_point.reasoning: 47.0s enters mid-pour with the cup already tipped and stream in the air — no approach, no dead setup frames; the shot opens on visible action so the 1.4s window is all pour → drops, matching the text read.

beats.beat_description: Food processor running, cranberries tumbling (side view)

camera.reasoning: Front because the side-view window shows cranberries visibly tumbling and pink splatter building on the walls — overhead would only show an opaque lid, hiding the transformation that's the whole point of this beat.

duration.reasoning: 1.5s holds long enough to register three distinct stages of blending (tumbling → blurred churn → pink splatter) so the viewer sees transformation, not just "machine on."

in_point.reasoning: 16.1s lands mid-churn where motion blur and ingredient blending are at peak intensity — the speed-ramped found moment of the mixture turning pink, which is the organic flourish worth anchoring on.

beats.beat_description: Cream cheese blocks in stand mixer with paddle

camera.reasoning: Front — held camera run continuing from the previous front shot (B19I3923.mov) into the next front shot (B19I3926.mov); this is mid-sequence hold, not a switch point, and front shows the paddle engagement with the cheese blocks at eye-level in a way overhead would flatten into a indistinguishable white mass.

duration.reasoning: 1.0s covers the full micro-action arc — paddle lowered → positioned → first rotation breaking cheese — which is one beat at ingredient-dump pacing, and the 101.391% ramp barely compresses it (effectively real-time).

in_point.reasoning: 9.3s is the "new scene" cut-in where the paddle first appears lowered into frame — starting here lands on the clean setup moment before motion, giving the viewer a readable establishing frame before the paddle rotates.

beats.effects_reasoning: Speed ramp at 101.391% is functional, not organic — 1.4% is imperceptible as a transformation ramp and is almost certainly a duration-fit nudge to hit the next beat cleanly; a true organic flourish would ramp far more aggressively (e.g., 150-400%) to sell a transformation.

beats.beat_description: Mixer paddle mid-rotation breaking up cheese
Whipped cream cheese in stand mixer bowl

camera.reasoning: Front is a held-camera continuation from the previous front shot (B19I3926.mov) — the editor is sitting on front through the whipping action rather than alternating, and this frame is the best-take moment showing the silky whipped texture resolve in the bowl before switching to overhead for the spreading nest.

duration.reasoning: 1.1s is the minimum needed to register the finished silky texture as a distinct beat from the prior whipping motion — any shorter and the "done" read is lost, any longer and it stalls the handoff to the overhead spreading nest.

in_point.reasoning: 47.2s catches the exact frame where the paddle's motion blur reveals fully-whipped, glossy cream cheese (not mid-whip chunks) — it's the payoff frame of the transformation, so the cut lands on the visual result rather than the process.

beats.beat_description: Dollop of whipped cream cheese on ceramic plate

camera.reasoning: Overhead because this beat is the start of a nested stop-motion spreading sequence (6 inner clips) — you cannot read a cream cheese disc forming into a circular base from eye-level, so overhead is the only angle that shows the shape/spread construction; this is an anchor moment, not a held-camera continuation.

duration.reasoning: 3.4s is long because the nest itself contains 6 progressive spreading stages (dollop → mound → spatula enters → widening → well → finished hero) — the duration is set by the internal stop-motion rhythm, not an external beat, and it needs time to land the transformation reveal.

in_point.reasoning: 0.0s because the nest was built from the top — the first inner clip is the clean dollop landing on the empty plate, which is the required "before" state for the stop-motion transformation to read.

beats.effects: Animated zoom in (100.0% -> 120.0%); Reframed (offset h:-0.50 v:-0.50)
beats.nested: Nested sequence 'Nest - spreading cheese' with 6 inner clips
beats.effects_reasoning: Organic flourish treatment — the 100→120% zoom push is the stop-motion equivalent of a speed ramp, driving energy INTO the finished disc reveal, while the h:-0.50 v:-0.50 reframe corrects the off-center framing (chips plate hanging off right edge, towel in bottom-left) so the plate centers as it zooms.

beats.beat_description: Dollop of cranberry salsa placed on cream cheese

camera.reasoning: Front because this is the switch point — the previous nest held overhead through the cream cheese spread, and front is the best take for showing the dollop's vertical landing onto the flat cream cheese bed (overhead would flatten that drop action into a shape change).

duration.reasoning: 0.8s because the dollop placement is a single punctuation beat — long enough to register "salsa has arrived on the cheese" before the spatula action takes over, short enough to not linger on a static dollop.

in_point.reasoning: 98.1s lands on the moment the dollop has just been deposited and the spoon is lifting away — the cleanest frame of first contact, skipping the approach and stopping before the spread begins in the next cut.

camera.reasoning: Front continues a held-camera run from the previous 0.8s front shot (B19I3935.mov) — the slotted spatula's spreading is a lateral smearing motion across the cream cheese bed, and front sells that horizontal gesture plus the widening salsa footprint, whereas overhead would flatten it into an abstract red blob on white.

duration.reasoning: 1.2s covers exactly one "spread widens" action beat — long enough to register the salsa expanding on the cream cheese, short enough to cut before the spatula swap at 26.5s (solid spatula patting flat), keeping this as a single discrete gesture rather than a multi-action blob.

in_point.reasoning: 115.5s catches the spatula mid-sweep with salsa actively widening on the cream cheese bed — earlier would show the spatula approaching empty, later would land in the smoothing/finishing phase; this is the clean "spread is happening now" frame where the transformation is most visible.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Finished cranberry salsa layer close-up → Same framing, cilantro sprinkled on top → Wide pull-back of plated dip on marble counter
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

camera.reasoning: Front because this is beat 13 in an extended front-camera hold during the ingredient dump run (rule 3) — there's no reason to switch to overhead mid-hold, and the eye-level angle lets the green cilantro pop against the red salsa as it lands.

duration.reasoning: 1.3s matches the read time of the short "Fresh cilantro" MOGRT — two words read quickly, so the hold is brief and the cut moves on before the text overstays.

in_point.reasoning: 7.1s is the moment cilantro is actively landing/settling on the salsa surface — the sprinkle action is visible and the finished layer reads as "complete" with the garnish now on it.

beats.beat_description: Wide pull-back of plated dip on marble counter

camera.reasoning: Front because this is the anchor shot for the money-shot reveal — a front-angle "hero pull-out" that shows the finished dip and the chip plate beside it in a composed, eye-level frame; overhead would flatten it into another top-down ingredient shot and kill the "finished dish" read.

duration.reasoning: 2.2s because this is a hero beat, not an ingredient beat — it has to hold long enough for the pull-back to land, the viewer to register the finished composition, and the hand to enter at ~0.5s in to motivate the next cut; the neighboring 1.3s beats would truncate the reveal.

in_point.reasoning: 79.2s because that's the frame where the pull-back has opened up enough to show the full plated composition with the chip plate in frame but before the hand arrives — the cleanest "finished dish" read, after which the hand entry at ~+0.5s carries momentum into the next shot.

beats.beat_description: Wide hero shot of finished dip with cilantro

camera.reasoning: Front is the anchor here — a macro, shallow-focus hero of the finished cranberry salsa with cilantro garnish, where the layered depth and out-of-focus foreground/background only read on the eye-level lens; overhead would flatten it into a texture map.

duration.reasoning: 1.1s holds the beauty shot long enough to register the garnish and focus fall-off without stalling, and sets up the release into the longer 1.8s overhead eating payoff that follows.

in_point.reasoning: 33.0s lands mid-drift with the cilantro already in frame and the macro focus settled, skipping the dead wind-in of the source clip so the cut opens on motion-already-in-progress rather than a static start.

beats.beat_description: Two hands dipping chips from opposite sides

camera.reasoning: Overhead is the anchor here — two hands entering symmetrically from opposite sides is a composition that only reads top-down, and after a front shot of a loaded chip it's the switch point that delivers the payoff of the dip being shared/used.

duration.reasoning: 1.8s covers the full action arc visible in the frames — hands meeting at the dip, one chip lifting with a drip, chip exiting frame — long enough to register the symmetry beat without overstaying before the front-angle end-card.

in_point.reasoning: 3.5s lands the cut exactly on the symmetrical moment — both hands already in frame dipping from opposite sides — skipping any approach/setup so the shot opens on its strongest composition.

beats.effects_reasoning: 68% is a functional tightening, not an organic flourish — cropping in removes plate/table edges and centers the two-hands-meeting composition so the symmetry reads cleanly at this duration.

beats.beat_description: Hand lifting chip with dip loaded up close

camera.reasoning: Front is the switch point after the overhead lift-out — this is the end-card anchor where the hero chip with loaded dip sits at eye-level so "the kitchn" logo has a clean top-left corner to animate into, something the overhead's top-down geometry couldn't accommodate.

duration.reasoning: 3.0s holds the end card long enough for the logo lock-up to animate in (~0.5s), settle (~1.5s), and read (~1.0s) before the video ends — it's the final hold, not a beat-matched cut.

in_point.reasoning: 13.2s lands right as the hand arrives at its hero position with the dip fully loaded and cilantro visible, one frame before the logo animation triggers — the cleanest version of the chip before the end-card lockup takes over.
